videos.title: Copilot Studio NEW Workflows 🚀 Full Tutorial | AI Automation Reimagined
videos.description:
Microsoft just dropped a brand new Workflows designer inside Copilot Studio — and this changes everything.

For the first time, AI-native actions like Agents, Prompts, Classify, and M365 Copilot sit alongside traditional Power Automate building blocks like Connectors, Loops, Variables, and If/Else — all in one canvas. This is a fusion between agent flows and power automate and we have a new designer interface! No switching between tools. Just one unified designer where AI and automation blocks a

videos.title: Keep up or get left behind
videos.description:
Keeping up with tech trends? You don't have to!

It's tough to stay updated in the fast-paced world of AI. Instead of trying to track everything, focus on what matters.

Assign team members to monitor specific areas. If something lasts over a month, it’s worth your attention.

In the AIverse, a month is a long time!

#AI #TechTrends #Innovation

videos.title: Microsoft's Rapid Rebuild Cycle
videos.description:
Is your product ready for a complete rebuild?

In today's fast-paced tech world, rebuilding from scratch is becoming the norm.
What used to take years can now be done in just a couple of months!
Embrace the change and stay ahead of the curve.

Are you ready to adapt?

#TechTrends
#ProductDevelopment
#Innovation

videos.title: Beating the AI Doom Cycle
videos.description: AI inequality explored as access to frontier models becomes scarce and selectively allocated by security, compute, and government controls. The role of Mythos, distillation, and recent pricing shifts demonstrates how token scarcity and stricter KYC concentrate capabilities among major firms and allied states. Policy responses include security hardening, rapid data-center buildouts, international compute partnerships, and contingency strategies for middle powers to avoid a haves-and-have-nots fut

videos.title: Why Agents Still Need Humans
videos.description:
NLW explores the next wave of human-agent collaboration, using Dan Shipper’s “After Automation” essay and Every’s agent experiments to argue that automation is creating more expert human work, not less. The episode looks at shared team agents, the “human sandwich” model, the limits of fully autonomous OpenClaw-style agents, and why Codex and Claude Code point toward a more semi-synchronous future of managing agent work across devices.
After Automation: ⁠https://every.to/p/after-automation

The A
